one_liner: NLP semantic entropy to medical VLM radiology report generation uncertainty
problem: Kuhn 2023 semantic entropy quantifies LLM answer uncertainty at semantic rather than token level but has never been applied to medical report generation. Current report UQ only examines token probabilities missing semantic equivalence.
approach: 1.Apply semantic entropy on CheXpert/IU X-Ray report generation via multi-sampling plus NLI clustering plus entropy. 2.Compare against token-entropy baseline verify semantic entropy better identifies hallucinated reports. 3.Validate against radiologist-annotated error reports.
why_new: Semantic entropy (Kuhn et al. NeurIPS 2023) is SOTA for LLM hallucination detection; medical report generation has zero applications. Distinction from C029: core mechanism is semantic equivalence class entropy not BERTScore consistency.
source_method: NLP semantic entropy (Kuhn et al. NeurIPS 2023)

one_liner: Ecological SDM epistemic uncertainty propagation to multimodal medical fusion UQ
problem: In multimodal fusion (dermoscopy plus clinical metadata) there is no theoretical framework for how uncertainty propagates across modalities. Ecological SDMs have mature multi-covariate epistemic uncertainty propagation formulas.
approach: 1.Build fusion classifier on ISIC2020 plus fitzpatrick17k skin-type metadata; use MC-Dropout to separately estimate per-modality epistemic uncertainty. 2.Apply SDM uncertainty propagation formula to combine two streams. 3.Verify combined UQ is better calibrated than simple summation.
why_new: SDM epistemic uncertainty propagation (Thuiller 2019) mature in ecology; multimodal medical AI has no corresponding framework. Existing multimodal fusion UQ does weighted averaging without propagation analysis.
source_method: ecological SDM epistemic uncertainty propagation (Thuiller et al. 2019)

one_liner: Recommender-system calibrated uncertainty-aware ranking to medical AI differential diagnosis top-k list
problem: Medical AI top-k diagnosis lists rank by softmax score only ignoring per-diagnosis uncertainty. Recommender-system uncertainty-aware ranking has never been applied to differential diagnosis list presentation.
approach: 1.Re-rank MC-Dropout/ensemble top-k lists on HAM10000 (7 classes) and APTOS retinopathy using uncertainty-penalized scores. 2.Define uncertainty-aware NDCG metric. 3.Validate clinical reasonableness with ground-truth labels.
why_new: Calibrated ranking (Bellogín 2017, Guo 2021 IRE) mature in recommender systems; nobody applied it to differential diagnosis UQ. Distinction from Top-K uncertainty work: ranking plus uncertainty penalty rather than select/abstain.
source_method: recommender system calibrated uncertainty-aware ranking (Bellogín and Castells 2017)

one_liner: Quantum information density matrix to entangled annotator disagreement modeling for medical multi-rater labels proof-of-concept
problem: Modeling multi-annotator disagreement with classical independent Bayesian distributions loses shared uncertainty structure. Quantum density matrices can represent entangled mixed states and may encode this more compactly.
approach: 1.Encode k-annotator classifications of n images as density matrices (k by k per image). 2.Use von Neumann entropy as ambiguity score compare to inter-annotator entropy. 3.Pilot on BraTS 3-rater subset.
why_new: Density matrix modeling has NLP/IR literature (Sordoni 2013, Li 2022 quantum IR) but zero applications to medical annotation uncertainty. Pilot is near-zero GPU (pure matrix algebra); high-B but low failure cost.
source_method: quantum information density matrix (Sordoni et al. 2013; Li and Witte 2022 quantum IR)
